'''Thomas Weaver''' is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
===2023===
Weaver made his NRL debut in Round 25 against the [[Penrith Panthers]] in a 14–40 loss.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-08-18 |title=Titans debutant No.7 Weaver ready for Panthers baptism |url=https://www.espn.co.uk/nrl/story/_/id/38212341/nrl-titans-debutant-no7-weaver-ready-panthers-baptism |access-date=2023-09-22 |website=ESPN}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Weaver was announced as one of eight players farewelled by the Titans.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-16 |title=Titans thank departing players in '25 |url=https://www.titans.com.au/news/2025/09/16/titans-thank-departing-players-in-25/ |access-date=2025-09-16 |website=Gold Coast Titans |language=en}}</ref>

On 24 October, he signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=24 October 2025 |title=Castleford sign Gold Coast half-back Weaver |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c986g8g4eqlo |access-date=24 October 2025 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Weaver said, "I wanted to start and run my own team and be the half-back," an opportunity offered by Castleford in a reshaped squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=McAllister |first=Josh |date=9 February 2026 |title=Tom Weaver ready to lead Castleford Tigers into new era after bold leap into Super League |url=https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/tom-weaver-castleford-tigers-leap-1650279 |access-date=28 February 2026 |website=All Out Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=14 February 2026 |title=Weaver ready to roar for Tigers |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p0n13s1y |access-date=28 March 2026 |website=BBC Radio Leeds}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
Weaver was assigned Castleford's number 7 shirt for the [[2026 Castleford Tigers season|2026 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=3 February 2026 |title=Castleford Tigers new boy is happy to be out of his comfort zone after swapping Australia for England |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-new-boy-is-happy-to-be-out-of-his-comfort-zone-after-swapping-australia-for-england-5500395 |access-date=28 February 2026 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He made his debut against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the [[2026 Challenge Cup|Challenge Cup]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=7 February 2026 |title=Doncaster 0 Castleford Tigers 14: Not picture perfect but job done for Ryan Carr |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/doncaster-0-castleford-tigers-14-not-picture-perfect-but-job-done-for-ryan-carr-5507339 |access-date=28 February 2026 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref> In round 3 of Super League, Weaver scored two tries and kicked five goals in Castleford's win against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Aloia |first=Andrew |date=27 February 2026 |title=Castleford beat Giants for first Super League win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2drzj29nno |access-date=28 February 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He contributed 16 points in their round 6 victory against [[Bradford Bulls]], including a show-and-go try under the posts.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harby |first=Chris |date=26 March 2026 |title=Qareqare hat-trick fires Castleford past Bradford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cnv83618rrno |access-date=28 March 2026 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
